criteria_verification.evidence: Created src/forge/cli/_serve_deps_stage_log.py with build_stage_log_recorder(sqlite_pool: _StageLogWriter) -> StageLogRecorder. Verified via isinstance(recorder, StageLogRecorder) since the Protocol is @runtime_checkable. criteria_verification.criterion_text: A round-trip test (write then read on the same pool) observes
criteria_verification.evidence: TestRoundTripWriteThenRead::test_record_running_then_read_observes_row constructs a real SqliteLifecyclePersistence facade against a freshly-migrated SQLite database (via connect_writer + migrations.apply_at_boot in tmp_path), seeds a builds row, calls recorder.record_running(...) with the dispatcher's payload shape, then calls persistence.read_stages(build_id) on the same pool and asserts that exactly one StageLogEntry is observed with build_id, stage_label=AUTOBUILD, target_kind='subagent', target_identifier=feature_id, status='PASSED', and details preserving correlation_id/subagent/task_id/feature_id plus the lifecycle_state='running' marker. The schema's CHECK constraint on stage_log.status only allows {PASSED, FAILED, GATED, SKIPPED}, so the Protocol's documented 'running' marker lives on details_json[lifecycle_state] (documented in AUTOBUILD_RUNNING_STATUS / AUTOBUILD_LIFECYCLE_STATE_KEY constants). Test passes.
criteria_verification.notes: TestRoundTripWriteThenRead::test_record_running_then_read_observes_row constructs a real SqliteLifecyclePersistence facade against a freshly-migrated SQLite database (via connect_writer + migrations.apply_at_boot in tmp_path), seeds a builds row, calls recorder.record_running(...) with the dispatcher's payload shape, then calls persistence.read_stages(build_id) on the same pool and asserts that exactly one StageLogEntry is observed with build_id, stage_label=AUTOBUILD, target_kind='subagent', target_identifier=feature_id, status='PASSED', and details preserving correlation_id/subagent/task_id/feature_id plus the lifecycle_state='running' marker. The schema's CHECK constraint on stage_log.status only allows {PASSED, FAILED, GATED, SKIPPED}, so the Protocol's documented 'running' marker lives on details_json[lifecycle_state] (documented in AUTOBUILD_RUNNING_STATUS / AUTOBUILD_LIFECYCLE_STATE_KEY constants). Test passes.
